headings.text: Year vs. Max Depth · 47 documented expeditions

paragraphs.text: An aggregated dashboard of environmental telemetry, recorded expeditions, and seabed coverage. Three charts compose this view: a five-axis spider chart across depth zones, a 47-point scatter of documented research dives, and a donut quantifying how much of the seafloor humans have actually mapped.
Each tick represents a single descent. Vertical position encodes maximum depth reached; amber dots mark missions exceeding 6,000m (hadal class).
All polygons rendered at 30% fill opacity overlaid in a single SVG group, normalized to a 0–1 axis per metric.

As of Q1 2024, the GEBCO/Seabed 2030 initiative has produced bathymetric coverage of roughly one-fifth of Earth's ocean floor at modern resolution (≥800m grid). The remaining 79.1% remains charted only by satellite altimetry-derived gravity inference.
An interactive infographic atlas of pressure, light, and life across the column of the world ocean. All charts rendered as inline SVG with clipped depth bands and static path geometry.
